title: Sutera antirrhinoides
intro: Sutera antirrhinoides, also known as the mock orange or blue throatwort, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Asia and Australia, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Sutera antirrhinoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sutera antirrhinoides is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, as well as being used in the production of tonics and syrups.
taxonomy: Sutera antirrhinoides is classified within the family Scrophulariaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as figworts or snapdragons. Sutera antirrhinoides is native to South Africa and is also known as Cape forget-me-not. The plant is an annual herb, belonging to the genus Sutera. It is a member of the order Lamiales in the kingdom Plantae.
